skill.name: baoyu-comic
skill.description: Create educational knowledge comics from source material with structured analysis, storyboard variants, character references, image prompts, and PDF export.
skill.summary: Turn source material into structured educational comics with styles, layouts, prompts, and PDF output.
skill.author: JimLiu

security_audit.summary: AI review found no evidence of malicious intent, prompt injection, credential access, network exfiltration, or hidden command execution. Most static high-risk hits are false positives from Markdown templates and visual style language, but the skill does instruct agents to run an external npx/Bun command and perform local file operations. Publish with a warning about local filesystem writes, page deletion or renaming, and runtime package execution for PDF export.

medium_findings.title: External PDF Merge Command Requires Runtime Execution
medium_findings.description: The skill instructs agents to run an npx command that launches Bun and executes the bundled PDF merge script. This is a legitimate feature, but it can trigger package download and local code execution, so users should approve it before use.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The command is explicitly documented and tied to PDF generation, with no evidence of obfuscation or malicious arguments. The risk is real because npx and Bun execute code in the user environment.

medium_findings.title: Local Filesystem Writes, Renames, and Deletions
medium_findings.description: The workflow creates analysis, storyboard, prompt, image, and PDF files, backs up existing directories, and supports page deletion or renumbering. These operations are expected for comic generation but can alter user files inside the selected target directory.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The instructions and script show concrete local file reads and writes. The scope appears limited to user-selected comic directories, so this is moderate operational risk rather than malicious behavior.

low_findings.title: Static High-Risk Keyword Matches Are False Positives
low_findings.description: The weak cryptography, C2, and reconnaissance alerts are caused by ordinary Markdown content such as visual metaphors, color names, layout descriptions, and examples. No cryptographic code, C2 endpoint, system probing, or network reconnaissance was found in reviewed files.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited lines are plain instructional prose for image composition and style. They do not contain executable code, network calls, cryptographic APIs, or system inspection logic.

low_findings.title: Protected Character Defaults May Create Publication Risk
low_findings.description: The Ohmsha mode defaults to named third-party characters for educational manga. This is not a code security issue, but marketplace users should replace them with original characters for public or commercial work.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The referenced names are explicitly listed as defaults in multiple files. The risk is content and rights-related, not evidence of malicious software behavior.

dangerous_patterns.title: Documented Shell Command Invocation
dangerous_patterns.description: The skill includes a shell command for PDF export using npx and Bun. This pattern should remain user-visible and require normal command approval.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The command is directly present in the skill instructions. Its purpose is clear, but executing package-managed commands is still a sensitive pattern.

dangerous_patterns.title: Synchronous Local File Reads During PDF Creation
dangerous_patterns.description: The merge script reads generated image files from disk before embedding them into a PDF. It does not send data over the network or invoke child processes.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The script uses local file APIs and pdf-lib to create output. The behavior is bounded but still requires filesystem access to user-selected content.

content.user_title: Create Educational Comic Storyboards and PDFs
content.value_statement: Complex source material is hard to turn into clear visual lessons. This skill guides Claude, Codex, and Claude Code through analysis, storyboards, character references, image prompts, and PDF assembly.
actual_capabilities: Analyzes source material for audience, themes, narrative arcs, and visual opportunities.
Creates three storyboard variants with different narrative approaches, styles, and layouts.
Builds character definition documents and reference sheet prompts for visual consistency.
Generates page-level image prompts for covers and sequential comic pages.
Supports multiple styles, layouts, aspect ratios, and output languages.
Merges generated PNG or JPG comic pages into a PDF using the included script.
limitations: Image creation depends on an available image generation tool or skill.
PDF export requires running a local npx and Bun command.
The skill does not automatically verify facts, citations, or historical accuracy.
Default named characters in Ohmsha mode should be replaced for public publication.

use_cases.title: Convert Lessons Into Comics
use_cases.description: Turn a lecture, article, or tutorial into a visual comic outline with panels, dialogue, and image prompts.

use_cases.title: Create Biography Comics
use_cases.description: Adapt a historical or professional biography into storyboards with character arcs and style guidance.

use_cases.title: Prototype Visual Explainers
use_cases.description: Create comic-style explainers for technical concepts, workflows, or research findings before final design work.

prompt_templates.title: Beginner Comic From Article
prompt_templates.scenario: Start with a single source document and default settings.
prompt_templates.prompt: Create an educational comic from this article. Use the default style and layout. Make the story clear for beginners.

prompt_templates.title: Choose Style and Audience
prompt_templates.scenario: Control the reader level and visual direction.
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a knowledge comic for high school students. Use a warm style, standard layout, and Chinese text.

prompt_templates.title: Technical Tutorial Comic
prompt_templates.scenario: Explain a complex concept with visual metaphors.
prompt_templates.prompt: Turn this machine learning tutorial into a comic. Use the tech style, dense layout, and include strong visual metaphors.

prompt_templates.title: Advanced Multi-Variant Workflow
prompt_templates.scenario: Compare story structures before generating pages.
prompt_templates.prompt: Analyze this biography and create three storyboard variants. Include character references, page prompts, and a PDF-ready file plan.

output_examples.input: A short article about Alan Turing and early computing.
output: Audience and theme analysis for a biography comic.
Three storyboard options with different styles and layouts.
Character reference guidance for Turing and supporting figures.
Cover and page prompts ready for image generation.

output_examples.input: A tutorial explaining neural networks to beginners.
output: A visual metaphor plan for nodes, data flow, and training.
A page-by-page comic structure with panels and dialogue.
Image prompts using an educational manga or tech style.

output_examples.input: Generated comic page images in a project folder.
output_examples.output: A single PDF comic assembled from numbered cover and page images.

best_practices: Use a dedicated output directory so generated files, backups, and PDFs stay isolated.
Review storyboards and character references before generating final images.
Replace protected character defaults with original characters for public projects.
anti_patterns: Do not run the PDF merge command without reviewing the target directory and output path.
Do not use pasted source material without checking rights and factual accuracy.
Do not generate all final pages before confirming style, language, and aspect ratio.

faq.question: Does this skill generate images by itself?
faq.answer: No. It prepares prompts and workflow steps for an available image generation tool or skill.

faq.question: Can it create comics in Chinese or English?
faq.answer: Yes. It detects source language and can generate storyboards and prompts in the selected language.

faq.question: What files does it create?
faq.answer: It can create analysis notes, storyboards, character references, page prompts, generated images, and a final PDF.

faq.question: Is the PDF export safe?
faq.answer: It uses a local script to read page images and write a PDF. Review the command before running it.

faq.question: Can I choose a custom art style?
faq.answer: Yes. The skill supports listed styles and custom natural language style descriptions.

faq.question: Should I use the default Ohmsha characters?
faq.answer: Use original replacement characters for public or commercial work to avoid rights concerns.
